attractions.name: Abraham Lincoln Capital Airport
attractions.description: Abraham Lincoln Capital Airport is a multi-use civil-military facility that serves as the primary aerial gateway to central Illinois. It offers commercial flights, general aviation services, and is home to the Illinois Air National Guard's 183rd Wing. Wikipedia
attractions.days_open: Daily (7 days a week) Official Site
highlights: Named the top primary airport in Illinois in 2020 by the Illinois Department of Transportation. Wikipedia
Home to the 183rd Wing of the Illinois Air National Guard. Wikipedia
Features the "SkyClub," a membership-based lounge for frequent travelers. Official Site
Includes an on-site solar farm, establishing itself as a "future-focused" airport. Hanson Inc.
Offers free parking and free wireless internet for all passengers. Official Site

attractions.name: Abraham Lincoln Presidential Library and Museum
attractions.description: The Abraham Lincoln Presidential Library and Museum documents the life of the 16th U.S. president and the course of the American Civil War through a combination of rigorous scholarship and high-tech showmanship Official Website. It houses an unparalleled collection of roughly 13 million historical items, serving as a premier center for research and public education on the legacy of Abraham Lincoln and Illinois history Wikipedia.
attractions.address: 212 N. Sixth St., Springfield, IL 62701 (Museum); 112 N. Sixth St., Springfield, IL 62701 (Library) Official Website
attractions.days_open: Museum: Daily; Library: Monday–Friday Official Website
highlights: Journey 1: The Pre-Presidential Years walkthrough exhibit Official Website
Journey 2: The White House Years walkthrough exhibit Official Website
Ghosts of the Library holographic presentation Official Website
Lincoln’s Eyes theater experience with special effects Official Website
Unsinkable Freedom and The Second American Revolution exhibits Official Website

attractions.name: Anheuser-Busch Brewery Anheuser-Busch's St. Louis brewery
attractions.category: Historic Site, Landmark, Brewery Missouri Division of Tourism, Wikipedia
attractions.description: The Anheuser-Busch Brewery in St. Louis is a National Historic Landmark and the flagship brewery of the Budweiser brand, founded in 1852. Visitors can explore the 142-acre campus, featuring stunning red-brick Romanesque architecture, historic aging tanks, and the world-famous Budweiser Clydesdales stables. Explore St. Louis, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 1200 Lynch Street, St. Louis, MO 63118 Anheuser-Busch's St. Louis brewery
attractions.city: St. Louis Anheuser-Busch's St. Louis brewery
attractions.days_open: Every Day Anheuser-Busch's St. Louis brewery
attractions.phone: 314-577-2626 or 1-800-DIAL BUD (1-800-342-5283) BUDWEISER TOURS CONTACT US
highlights: Historic Budweiser Clydesdales Stables Any Adventure With You
The Historic Brew House with its intricate ironwork Wikipedia
Beechwood Aging Cellars Stlmotherhood
Beer tasting straight from the Finishing Cellars Explore St. Louis
The Biergarten for dining and beer samples Anheuser-Busch's St. Louis brewery
The Lyon Schoolhouse Museum on the brewery grounds Wikipedia

attractions.name: Augie's Front Burner
attractions.description: Augie's Front Burner was a premier fine-dining destination in downtown Springfield for over two decades, celebrated for its "casual dining with gourmet flare." Owned by Chef Augie Mrozowski, the restaurant was a favorite gathering spot for state legislators and was renowned for its commitment to the local food movement and seasonal produce.
attractions.address: 2 W Old State Capitol Plaza, Springfield, IL 62701
highlights: Gourmet, locally sourced fare featuring seasonal produce from the farmers market.
A popular "stomping ground" for Illinois state legislators and political figures.
Located in the historic Old State Capitol Plaza in the heart of downtown.
Renowned for unique dishes like the Pork Belly Stir-Fry and the Springfield specialty "Horseshoe."
Led by local culinary icon Augie Mrozowski, who spent over 50 years in the industry.
Offered a distinctive atmosphere described as a "blend of experiences engaging all senses."

attractions.name: Bakers & Hale Bakers & Hale
attractions.description: Bakers & Hale is a locally owned and operated "seed-to-table" restaurant that opened in August 2018. It is proud to be one of the only farm-to-table restaurants in the St. Louis Metro East, featuring an on-site garden where many of its own vegetables and herbs are grown. Bakers & Hale
attractions.address: 7120 Montclair Ave, Godfrey, IL 62035 Bakers & Hale
attractions.days_open: Monday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Closed Tuesday) Bakers & Hale
highlights: Seed-to-table dining with ingredients from an on-site garden Bakers & Hale
Extensive live music calendar featuring local bands Bakers & Hale
Indoor and outdoor bar areas, including a pet-friendly patio Great Rivers & Routes
Focus on local suppliers like Marcoot Jersey Creamery and Duke Bakery Bakers & Hale
Unique "no-freezer" philosophy, using only a small home-sized refrigerator for fresh ingredients Bakers & Hale

attractions.name: Boone's (formerly Boone's Saloon) Boone's
attractions.category: Restaurant, Bar, and Entertainment Venue Visit Springfield
attractions.description: Boone's is a popular local pub and restaurant located in downtown Springfield near the Illinois State Capitol. Known for its expansive beer garden and live music, it offers a diverse menu featuring Springfield's famous horseshoes, tacos, and gourmet burgers, with a special breakfast menu served on weekends. Visit Springfield, Boone's
attractions.address: 301 West Edwards Street, Springfield, Illinois 62704 Boone's
highlights: Springfield's original style 'horseshoes' topped with signature cheese sauce Boone's Menu
Weekend breakfast featuring signature bacon and blueberry pancakes The Observer
Large outdoor beer garden and event hosting space Tripadvisor
Prime location within view of the Illinois State Capitol Visit Springfield
Extensive 'naanwich' and craft burger selection Boone's Menu
Regular live music and community events Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Cahokia Mounds State Historic Site Cahokia Mounds Official Site
attractions.category: State Historic Site, UNESCO World Heritage Site, Archaeological Site Cahokia Mounds Official Site
attractions.description: Cahokia Mounds State Historic Site preserves the remains of the largest and most sophisticated prehistoric native civilization north of Mexico. At its peak around AD 1100, this Mississippian metropolis spanned over six square miles and was home to a population of up to 20,000 people. Cahokia Mounds Official Site, UNESCO World Heritage Centre
attractions.address: 30 Ramey St, Collinsville, IL 62234 Cahokia Mounds Official Site
attractions.days_open: Grounds: Open Daily; Interpretive Center: Thursday–Sunday (Currently closed for renovations) Cahokia Mounds Official Site, Illinois Department of Natural Resources
highlights: Monks Mound: The largest prehistoric earthwork in the Americas, rising 100 feet high. Cahokia Mounds Official Site
Woodhenge: A reconstructed ancient solar calendar made of cedar posts. Cahokia Mounds Official Site
Interpretive Center: A world-class museum featuring life-size dioramas and artifacts (currently under renovation). Cahokia Mounds Official Site
Birdman Tablet: An iconic sandstone artifact depicting a masked dancer, serving as the site's logo. Cahokia Mounds Official Site
Grand Plaza: A massive cleared area once used for public gatherings and ceremonies. UNESCO World Heritage Centre

attractions.name: Camp Butler National Cemetery
attractions.description: Originally established as a Union Civil War training camp and later used as a Confederate prison, Camp Butler is one of the fourteen original national cemeteries authorized by President Abraham Lincoln in 1862. Today, it serves as a serene final resting place for more than 25,000 veterans and their families, including over 1,700 Union and Confederate soldiers (VA.gov, Enjoy Illinois).
highlights: Site of the second-largest Civil War training camp in Illinois (Wikipedia)
Historic Confederate burial section featuring unique pointed headstones (Tripadvisor)
Home to various memorials, including the Korean War Veterans Memorial and Illinois LST sailors monument (VA.gov)
Final resting place of Medal of Honor recipient Seaman John Catherwood (Tripadvisor)
Host of major annual events like Memorial Day ceremonies and Wreaths Across America (Airial Travel)

attractions.name: Carpenter Street Hotel
attractions.description: The Carpenter Street Hotel is a boutique downtown property situated in the heart of Springfield's Medical District. This smoke-free facility offers comfortable accommodations and is located within walking distance of major historical landmarks and medical centers. It is well-regarded for providing a convenient home base for both leisure and business travelers exploring Illinois' capital.
highlights: Complimentary hot breakfast buffet served daily.
Free high-speed wireless internet access in all guestrooms and public areas.
Complimentary airport and area shuttle service within a 5-mile radius.
Located just a 7-minute walk from the Abraham Lincoln Presidential Library and Museum.
On-site amenities include a fitness center, business center, and guest laundry facilities.
Fully ADA-compliant and non-smoking facility.

attractions.name: Charlie Parker's Diner
attractions.description: Charlie Parker's Diner is a locally owned, 1950s-themed restaurant housed in a historic Quonset hut that was established in 1992 (Charlie Parker's Diner). The diner is famous for its massive breakfast portions and has been featured on the Food Network's "Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives" (Yelp). It offers a nostalgic atmosphere and is widely recognized for award-winning breakfast items, including its signature 16-inch pancake (Charlie Parker's Diner Menu).
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed on Easter, Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's Day) (Charlie Parker's Diner)
highlights: Featured on Food Network\
Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
Charlie\'s Famous Giant 16-inch Pancake

National Grand Champion of the Thomas' Hometown Breakfast Battle (Charlie Parker's Diner)
Signature "Breakfast Shoe" and "Horseshoe" sandwiches, a Springfield local specialty ([Charlie Parker\

attractions.name: City Museum City Museum Official Site
attractions.category: Museum / All-ages Playground City Museum Official Site
attractions.description: Housed in a 600,000-square-foot former shoe warehouse, City Museum is an ever-evolving, artist-built playground made of repurposed architectural and industrial objects. It features a surrealistic landscape of slides, tunnels, and secret passages designed for adventurous visitors of all ages. City Museum Official Site, Roadtrippers
attractions.address: 750 North 16th Street, St. Louis, MO 63103 Explore St. Louis
attractions.city: St. Louis City Museum Official Site
attractions.days_open: Wednesday through Sunday (Closed Monday and Tuesday) Tripadvisor
highlights: 10-story spiral slide (Shoe Shafts) Wikipedia)
MonstroCity outdoor climbing structure with two suspended aircraft Wikipedia)
Enchanted Caves running through the center of the museum Wikipedia)
Rooftop Ferris Wheel and a school bus jutting off the edge Wikipedia)
The Artquarium (formerly World Aquarium) Wikipedia)
World's Largest Pencil The Wandering Whittens

attractions.name: Clayville Historic Site, Official Website
attractions.category: Historic Site, Open-air Museum, Wikipedia, Facebook
attractions.description: Clayville Historic Site features the 1824 Broadwell Inn & Tavern, the oldest brick building in Sangamon County, which served as a stagecoach stop between Springfield and Beardstown. The site includes a reconstructed pioneer village with log cabins, a one-room schoolhouse, and an agricultural museum that showcases 19th-century frontier life through volunteer-led demonstrations and seasonal festivals, Visit Springfield, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 12828 State Route 125, Pleasant Plains, IL 62677, Visit Springfield, Enjoy Illinois
highlights: Broadwell Inn & Tavern (1824), the oldest brick building in Sangamon County, Visit Springfield
Batterton Cabin and other relocated 19th-century pioneer structures, Illinois Times
Agricultural museum in an old barn featuring artifacts like a trunk from the Donner Party, Tripadvisor
Seasonal festivals featuring blacksmithing, weaving, and period music, Clayville Official
One-room schoolhouse and authentic frontier artisan workshops, Airial Travel

attractions.name: Cozy Dog Drive In
attractions.description: Cozy Dog Drive In is a historic Route 66 landmark famous for being the birthplace of the corn dog on a stick, invented by Ed Waldmire Jr. in 1946. The restaurant features a nostalgic 1950s diner atmosphere filled with Route 66 memorabilia and is still family-owned and operated by the third generation of the Waldmire family (Enjoy Illinois, Visit Springfield Illinois).
highlights: Original "Cozy Dog" corn dog on a stick made with a secret 1946 batter recipe (Cozy Dog Drive In)
Extensive collection of Route 66 memorabilia, souvenirs, and artwork by Bob Waldmire (Wikipedia)
Nostalgic 1950s diner interior with a guest book for international travelers (Airial Travel)
Homemade fresh chili and white bean soup using original family spices (Yelp)
Fresh-cut French fries made daily from real potatoes (Yelp)

attractions.name: D'Arcy's Pint Official Website
attractions.description: D'Arcy's Pint is a cozy, neighborhood-style bar and restaurant in Springfield, Illinois, that has been serving authentic Irish fare and American classics since 1998. It is widely renowned for its world-famous horseshoes and provides a vibrant, friendly atmosphere reminiscent of the Emerald Isle. Official Website, Visit Springfield
attractions.address: 661 W Stanford Ave, Springfield, IL 62704 Official Website
attractions.days_open: Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday Official Website
highlights: World-famous Springfield Horseshoe sandwiches Illinois Times
Signature Irish Nachos and Potato Cabbage Soup Tripadvisor
Traditional Irish dishes including Shepherd's Pie and Corned Beef & Cabbage Official Website
Extensive beverage menu featuring a full bar and a variety of pints Official Website
Vibrant atmosphere with outdoor seating and live music events Official Website, Evendo

attractions.name: Dana-Thomas House (Susan Lawrence Dana House)
attractions.description: The Dana-Thomas House is a masterpiece of Prairie School architecture designed by Frank Lloyd Wright for philanthropist Susan Lawrence Dana between 1902 and 1904. It is one of the most complete and best-preserved examples of Wright's early "organic architecture," featuring a vast collection of original furniture and art glass integrated into the home's structure. Wikipedia, Illinois Department of Natural Resources
attractions.days_open: Daily (Monday–Sunday) Illinois Department of Natural Resources
highlights: Over 250 pieces of original art glass, including windows, skylights, and light fixtures Illinois Department of Natural Resources
More than 100 pieces of custom-designed Frank Lloyd Wright white oak furniture Wikipedia
Unique architectural features including 16 different floor elevations and a barrel-vaulted gallery ceiling Wikipedia
The iconic "Sumac" motif found in the art glass and a mural by George Mann Niedecken Wikipedia
A 35-room layout covering 12,000 square feet of living space Dana-Thomas House Foundation
An indoor "Torii gate" hallway inspired by Japanese aesthetics Wikipedia

attractions.name: Daughters of Union Veterans of the Civil War Museum
attractions.description: Founded in 1885, this museum preserves artifacts and history of the Civil War and the Grand Army of the Republic. It serves as the national headquarters for the Daughters of Union Veterans of the Civil War and offers visitors a glimpse into the lives of Union soldiers through an extensive collection of personal items and official records. Visit Springfield Illinois, American Battlefield Trust
highlights: Extensive collection of Civil War artifacts including rifles, medals, and actual battle swords.
Complete set of 'War of the Rebellion: Official Records of the Union and Confederate Armies.'
Original Union soldier uniforms and era-specific dresses on display.
Historical letters from soldiers at the front, currency, and musical instruments like drums.
The Dr. Benjamin Franklin Stephenson Library and Research Center, located in a 1898 American Foursquare house.
A memorial brick garden honoring founders, members, and veterans dedicated in 2010.

attractions.name: DreamMaker Bath & Kitchen of Springfield (formerly related to Level Five Kitchen and Bath)
attractions.description: DreamMaker Bath & Kitchen of Springfield is a full-service design-build remodeling company specializing in custom kitchen and bathroom renovations. The team provides a seamless experience from design through construction, focusing on high-quality craftsmanship and personalized service for homeowners in the Springfield area.
highlights: Full-service design-build remodeling process
Specialization in custom kitchen and bathroom designs
Professional design center for material selection
Expertise in cabinet installation and countertop updates
Remodeling services for basements, home offices, and laundry rooms

attractions.name: Edwards Place Historic Home
attractions.description: Built in 1833 and remodeled in 1857, Edwards Place is the oldest house in Springfield on its original foundation. This Italianate mansion serves as a historic house museum interpreting social and domestic life in antebellum Springfield and was a prominent social center for citizens like Abraham Lincoln and Stephen Douglas (Official Website, Enjoy Illinois).
attractions.days_open: Tuesday - Saturday (Closed January and February except by appointment) (Official Website)
highlights: Authentic "Lincoln Courting Couch" where Abraham Lincoln and Mary Todd sat during their courtship
Oldest house in Springfield remaining on its original foundation (built 1833)
Collection of Victorian furniture including pieces that belonged to the Edwards family
Seven portraits by renowned artist George P.A. Healy
Striking Italianate architecture featuring bracketed cornices and a cupola with a skylight
Part of the Springfield Art Association's historic three-acre campus

attractions.name: Edwin Watts Southwind Park
attractions.description: Southwind Park is an 80-acre, state-of-the-art facility designed as a national model for inclusivity and environmental excellence. It features 100% barrier-free accessibility for all ages and abilities, including unique attractions like a treeless treehouse and sensory gardens. Springfield Park District, Visit Springfield Illinois
highlights: Erin's Pavilion: A 15,000 sq ft LEED Platinum certified visitors center and rental facility. Springfield Park District
Treeless Treehouse: A fully accessible, barrier-free observation structure. Enjoy Illinois
Sensory Gardens: Five unique gardens, including an Enabling Garden with raised planter beds. Springfield Park District
Selvaggio Historic Arches: A set of arches featuring music and light shows. Visit Springfield Illinois
Great Lawn: An 8-acre grassy area perfect for kite flying and community events. Springfield Park District
Urban Trail & Navigation System: A 2.5-mile concrete trail with built-in directional guides. Springfield Park District

attractions.name: Elijah Iles House Official Website
attractions.description: Built circa 1837, the Elijah Iles House is the oldest residential structure in Springfield and a rare example of Greek Revival architecture in Central Illinois. The house serves as a museum featuring original black walnut woodwork, pre-Civil War furniture, and the Farrell and Ann Gay Museum of Springfield History. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 628 S. 7th Street, Springfield, IL 62703 Official Website
highlights: Oldest standing house in Springfield, dating back to approximately 1837. Wikipedia
Features original black walnut woodwork and a graceful original staircase. Tripadvisor
Houses the Farrell and Ann Gay Museum of Springfield History in the lower level. Official Website
Displays an extensive collection of pre-Civil War furniture on the upper levels. Official Website
Historically linked to Abraham Lincoln, who was a close friend of Elijah Iles. Tripadvisor
Listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 1978. Wikipedia

attractions.name: Enos Park Neighborhood EPNIA
attractions.description: Enos Park is the oldest and one of the most historically and architecturally significant neighborhoods in Springfield, Illinois. Located just north of downtown, it features a diverse collection of Victorian, Italianate, and Queen Anne style homes dating back to the early nineteenth century. Known as the "Jewel of Springfield," the area is currently undergoing revitalization efforts led by the Enos Park Neighborhood Improvement Association to preserve its rich heritage. EPNIA, Homes.com
attractions.address: Bounded by 2nd St, 10th St, Carpenter St, and North Grand Ave (Association: PO Box 2202, Springfield, IL 62705) EPNIA, UWCI
highlights: Historic Homes Walking Tour featuring over 30 architecturally significant residences EPNIA Walking Tour
Enos Park Sculpture Garden at 8th and Enos Ave EPNIA Banner
Enos Park Neighborhood Gardens at 1022 N 5th Street EPNIA Gardens
Edwards Place Historic Home and the Springfield Art Association Reddit
McClernand Public School, a historic educational landmark EPNIA History
Third Presbyterian Church and Kumler United Methodist Church EPNIA History

attractions.name: First Presbyterian Church of Springfield
attractions.description: First Presbyterian Church is a historic congregation established in 1828, famously known as the family church of Abraham and Mary Todd Lincoln. The current Gothic Revival building, completed in 1868, houses the original Lincoln family pew and seven unique Tiffany stained-glass windows. (Official Site, Wikipedia))
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Office), Sunday & Wednesday (Worship), Fridays in Summer (Tours) (Official Site, Official Site, Official Site)
highlights: Original Abraham Lincoln family pew (Pew 20) (Official Site)
Seven unique stained-glass windows designed by Tiffany Studios (Wikipedia))
John Brombaugh Opus 35 pipe organ specifically crafted for the sanctuary (Official Site)
Funeral site of Mary Todd Lincoln in 1882 (Wikipedia))
Site of early Springfield NAACP meetings following the 1908 Race Riot (Illinois Times)

attractions.name: Gabatoni's Restaurant and Cocktail Lounge
attractions.description: Gabatoni's Italian Restaurant is a long-standing family-owned eatery and cocktail lounge that has served the Springfield community since 1951. Known for its old-school atmosphere and authentic Italian-American cuisine, the restaurant specializes in thin-crust pizza, traditional pasta dishes, and hand-breaded sandwiches. Gabatoni's Restaurant, Springfield Business Journal
highlights: Famous 'EBA' (Everything But Anchovies) thin-crust pizza Gabatoni's Menu
Classic Italian-American specialties like the Poor Boy sandwich and Horseshoe Gabatoni's Menu
Over 70 years of history as a beloved neighborhood landmark BBB
Full-service cocktail lounge with an old-school, inviting atmosphere Gabatoni's Restaurant
Highly-rated local favorite, frequently named among the best pizza in Springfield Wanderlog

attractions.name: Gateway Arch National Park
attractions.category: National Park, Historic Site, Landmark, Park
attractions.description: Gateway Arch National Park commemorates Thomas Jefferson's vision of westward expansion and St. Louis's role as the "Gateway to the West." The park features the iconic 630-foot-tall stainless steel Gateway Arch, an underground museum exploring over 200 years of history, and the historic Old Courthouse National Park Service. Visitors can take a tram to the top of the arch for panoramic views of the city and the Mississippi River Gateway Arch Official Site.
attractions.days_open: Open daily year-round; closed on Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's Day Gateway Arch Hours.
highlights: Tram Ride to the Top for 30-mile panoramic views of St. Louis and the Mississippi River
Museum under the Gateway Arch featuring six interactive galleries on westward expansion
Historic Old Courthouse, the site of the landmark Dred Scott freedom trials
Scenic 91-acre park grounds with reflection ponds and five miles of paved paths
"Monument to the Dream" documentary film detailing the arch\

attractions.name: Horseshoe Sandwich (Springfield Signature Dish)
attractions.description: The Horseshoe is a legendary open-faced sandwich and the signature culinary dish of Springfield, Illinois. It consists of two thick slices of toasted bread (typically Texas toast) topped with a choice of meat, a mountain of crispy French fries, and a rich, creamy cheese sauce. Enjoy Illinois, Wikipedia
attractions.address: Various locations; originally created at the Leland Hotel (6th and Capitol, Springfield, IL)
attractions.days_open: Varies by restaurant (many open daily) Visit Springfield Illinois
attractions.phone: (217) 789-2360 (Visit Springfield Illinois office) Visit Springfield Illinois
highlights: Created in 1928 by Chef Joe Schweska at the Leland Hotel in Springfield. SangamonLink
Traditional recipe features thick-sliced ham cut in a horseshoe shape. Chowhound
Includes a "mountain" of French fries representing the nails of a horseshoe. Visit Springfield Illinois
Smothered in a proprietary cheese sauce, often a variation of Welsh rarebit. What's Cooking America
Available in a smaller portion known as the "Ponyshoe." Enjoy Illinois
Recognized as one of Illinois' most unique and famous sandwiches. Chowhound

attractions.name: Hospital Sisters of St. Francis Heritage Museum in Poverello Hall Hospital Sisters of St. Francis
attractions.category: Museum / Historic Site Hospital Sisters of St. Francis, Catholic Health Association
attractions.description: The Heritage Museum in Poverello Hall is a sophisticated museum located at the St. Francis Convent that outlines the 150-year history of the Hospital Sisters of St. Francis and their healing ministry in central Illinois and beyond. The museum features Franciscan imagery, stories, photographs, artifacts, and videos documenting the community's impact desde its founding in Germany in 1844 and arrival in Springfield in 1875. Hospital Sisters of St. Francis, Catholic Health Association
attractions.address: 4849 LaVerna Road, Springfield, IL 62707 Hospital Sisters of St. Francis
attractions.city: Springfield Hospital Sisters of St. Francis
highlights: Bronze sculpture depicting the sisters caring for Mary Todd Lincoln during her final years Catholic Health Association
The "Refectory" exhibit featuring a vintage table and tableware from various eras Hospital Sisters of St. Francis
A vintage desk with 12 drawers, each telling a story about one of the community's hospitals Hospital Sisters of St. Francis
Artifacts and letters from the first sisters who arrived in the U.S. in 1875 Hospital Sisters of St. Francis
Exhibit on foreign missions in China and Japan, including "Embracing the Leper" Hospital Sisters of St. Francis
The original "Nursing Sister" textbook and historical school of nursing artifacts Hospital Sisters of St. Francis

attractions.name: Illinois Korean War Memorial
attractions.description: The Illinois Korean War Memorial is a solemn tribute dedicated on June 16, 1996, honoring the 1,748 Illinoisans killed during the 1950-1953 Korean War (Illinois Department of Veterans Affairs). The monument's centerpiece is a 12-foot-tall bronze bell surrounded by four larger-than-life figures representing the branches of the armed services, including the Army, Navy/Coast Guard, Air Force, and Marines (Enjoy Illinois). Inscribed on the granite base are the names of the fallen soldiers, and a carillon system plays brief musical programs at regular intervals (Oak Ridge Cemetery).
highlights: 12-foot-tall bronze bell centerpiece mounted on a granite base (Enjoy Illinois)
Four larger-than-life bronze statues representing the Army, Navy/Coast Guard, Air Force, and Marines (Illinois Department of Veterans Affairs)
Names of 1,748 Illinoisans killed or missing in action inscribed on eight walls of the granite base (Enjoy Illinois)
A carillon system that plays brief musical programs at regular intervals (Oak Ridge Cemetery)
Inscribed quotations from each branch, including Marine General O.P. Smith's famous 'Retreat hell' quote (Illinois Department of Veterans Affairs)

attractions.name: Illinois State Capitol Building
attractions.category: Historic Site, Landmark, Government Building Enjoy Illinois, Wikipedia
attractions.description: The Illinois State Capitol is a grand architectural masterpiece in the French Renaissance and Italianate styles, featuring a majestic 361-foot dome that is the tallest non-skyscraper capitol dome in the United States. Completed in 1888, it serves as the seat of the Illinois General Assembly and houses the executive and legislative branches of the state government. Wikipedia, The Pasfield House
attractions.days_open: Monday through Sunday (Closed on New Year's Day, Easter Sunday, Thanksgiving, Friday after Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve, and Christmas Day) Tripadvisor
highlights: The majestic 361-foot dome, featuring a stained-glass replica of the state seal in its oculus. Wikipedia
Intricate legislative chambers for the House of Representatives and the Senate. The Pasfield House
The Hall of Governors, showcasing portraits of every Illinois governor. The Pasfield House
Historical statues on the grounds, including Abraham Lincoln and the Lincoln-Douglas Debate Memorial. The Pasfield House
A breathtaking grand staircase and ornate rotunda with a historical plaster frieze. The Pasfield House

attractions.name: Illinois State Fairgrounds Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.category: Fairgrounds, Historic Site, Entertainment Venue Wikipedia
attractions.description: The Illinois State Fairgrounds is a historic 366-acre site in Springfield, Illinois, renowned for hosting the annual Illinois State Fair since 1894. Listed on the National Register of Historic Places, the grounds feature over 100 permanent buildings, including a historic grandstand and a one-mile dirt racetrack. Beyond the fair, the site serves as a year-round venue for livestock shows, auto races, and various community events Wikipedia, Official Website
attractions.address: 801 E. Sangamon Ave., Springfield, IL 62706 Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.days_open: Daily during the Illinois State Fair (mid-August); Office open Monday-Friday year-round Official Website
highlights: Home to the famous 'Butter Cow' sculpture, a life-sized icon crafted from 500 pounds of butter since the 1920s Enjoy Illinois
Features 'Conservation World,' a 30-acre natural area with fishing, rolling hills, and outdoor education Enjoy Illinois
The 'Route 66 Experience' offers a microform walk of the iconic road and a neon sign park Enjoy Illinois
The 13,000-capacity Grandstand hosts major concerts and historic auto and horse racing events Wikipedia
Listed on the National Register of Historic Places with over 100 permanent buildings Wikipedia

attractions.name: Illinois State Fairgrounds Route 66 Experience
attractions.description: The Illinois State Fairgrounds Route 66 Experience is an educational, self-guided exhibit that highlights over 90 communities along the Illinois segment of the Mother Road. The attraction features a collection of vintage billboards, a drive-in movie theater display, and the Legends Neon Sign Park, providing a nostalgic journey from Chicago to the Chain of Rocks Bridge.
attractions.address: 801 E. Sangamon Ave, Springfield, IL 62794 (Inside Gate 2 at the corner of Route 66 and Sangamon Ave)
highlights: Legends Neon Sign Park featuring restored and replica neon signs
Retro drive-in movie theater display and vintage billboards
Tributes to iconic Illinois Muffler Men statues
Paved brick road section representing the historic highway pavement
Giant 25-foot tall fiberglass statue and interactive photo opportunities
Micro-version of the entire Illinois Route 66 corridor from Chicago to Madison

attractions.name: Illinois State Museum
attractions.category: Art, History, and Natural History Museum
attractions.description: The Illinois State Museum is a premier institution dedicated to the life, land, people, and art of Illinois. Founded in 1877, it provides visitors with world-renowned exhibits on natural history, anthropology, and heritage through three floors of permanent and rotating galleries. Illinois State Museum, Wikipedia
attractions.days_open: Daily (except New Year's Day, Thanksgiving, and Christmas) Illinois State Museum
highlights: Changes: Dynamic Illinois Environments - An exhibit tracing the environmental history of the state. Illinois State Museum
At Home in the Heartland - Explores household life in Illinois over the past 300 years. Illinois State Museum
Mary Ann MacLean Play Museum - A hands-on discovery area for children ages 18 months to grade 4. Illinois State Museum
America's Elephant - A 'Hot Science' gallery exhibit focused on the American mastodon. Illinois State Museum
Indigenous Beadwork of the Great Lakes - Featuring rare examples of 20th-century Native American artistry. Illinois State Museum
Minohsayaki ‘Painted Robes’ - A collaborative exhibit on historic hide-painting traditions. Illinois State Museum

attractions.name: Illinois State Museum-Dickson Mounds (Dickson Mounds Museum)
attractions.description: The Dickson Mounds Museum is a major on-site archaeological museum that explores 12,000 years of Native American history in the Illinois River Valley. It is a branch of the Illinois State Museum and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places, featuring innovative exhibits, artifacts, and preserved indigenous building remains.
highlights: River Valley Gallery: Explores the character of the Illinois River and human interaction with it since the Ice Age.
People of the Valley: Portrays a sequence of Indigenous cultures through artifacts, murals, and hands-on exhibits.
The Discovery Center: A learning place with interactive activities for visitors of all ages.
Eveland Village: Preserved excavated remains of three early Indigenous buildings (round, cross-shaped, and rectangular).
Outdoor Nature Trails: Series of walking trails through the museum grounds available during daylight hours.
In Flight: Rotating exhibition featuring watercolors of birds found in central Illinois by artist Peggy Macnamara.

attractions.name: Illinois Vietnam Veterans Memorial
attractions.description: Dedicated in 1988, the Illinois Vietnam Veterans Memorial honors the nearly 3,000 Illinois residents who were killed or listed as missing in action during the Vietnam War. The memorial features five black granite walls inscribed with names, radiating from a central eternal flame, with courtyards dedicated to each branch of the armed services Illinois Department of Veterans Affairs, Wikipedia.
highlights: Five black granite walls inscribed with the names of 2,970 fallen or missing Illinoisans IDVA
A central eternal flame burning atop the memorial where the granite walls converge Wikipedia
Five courtyards, each representing a different branch of the United States Armed Forces IDVA
Inscriptions of the names of the highest-decorated veterans and those listed as POW/MIA IDVA
An annual vigil and memorial service held each May and on Memorial Day Wikipedia

attractions.name: Incredibly Delicious
attractions.description: Incredibly Delicious is a locally owned French Bakery and Café located in the historic Weber House near downtown Springfield, Illinois. Founded in 1995, it specializes in artisanal breads, traditional croissants, handmade pastries, specialty cakes, and desserts.
highlights: Located in the historic Howard K. Weber House, a 19th-century Italianate mansion.
Specializes in artisanal breads, French pastries, and seasonal desserts.
Features an enchanting garden with outdoor patio seating.
Daily rotating breakfast and lunch menus made with fresh, local ingredients.
Multiple-time winner of 'Best Bakery' and 'Best Dessert' in Springfield.

attractions.name: Inn at 835 Boutique Hotel
attractions.description: The Inn at 835 is a nationally recognized historic boutique hotel and landmark originally designed by Bell Miller during the Arts and Crafts Movement. This stylish Classical Revival structure has been fully renovated to offer modern conveniences while preserving the gracious charm and refined elegance of the early 1900s.
highlights: Listed on the National Register of Historic Places Enjoy Illinois
Complimentary full Midwestern breakfast served 7 days a week Official Website
Freshly-baked chocolate chip cookies delivered to guest rooms each evening Tripadvisor
Period-accurate restoration featuring ornate oak woodwork, antiques, and inviting fireplaces Yelp
Evening appetizer reception in the parlor Tripadvisor
Within walking distance of major historic sites including the Illinois State Capitol Official Website

attractions.name: Interurban Trail
attractions.description: The Interurban Trail is a paved 8.3-mile rail-trail that follows the historic corridor of the abandoned Illinois Terminal Railroad. Stretching between Springfield and Chatham, the trail offers a blend of suburban and rural scenery through open farmland and wooded areas. It is a popular route for bicycle commuters and recreational users, providing a key link in the regional trail network. Wikipedia), Springfield Park District
attractions.address: Wabash Ave and S. Park Ave. Springfield Park District
highlights: Crossing Lake Springfield on a refurbished railroad bridge TrailLink/)
Paralleling the active Union Pacific Railroad tracks used by Amtrak Wikipedia)
Direct connection to the Wabash Trail in Springfield Springfield Park District
Scenic views of central Illinois farmland and rural landscapes TrailLink/)
Historic rail corridor once part of the electrified Illinois Terminal Railroad SangamonLink
Opportunities for wildlife viewing, including deer and wild turkeys YouTube

attractions.name: James' Home Kitchen (formerly part of the Chatham Road Restaurants and Diners search area) Official Website
attractions.description: James' Home Kitchen is a charming, locally owned eatery in Springfield that beautifully marries American and Pan-Asian flavors. The restaurant is Christian-owned and operated, featuring a family-friendly atmosphere in a newly renovated facility with a unique menu ranging from Texas BBQ to Asian fusion. Visit Springfield, Official Website
attractions.address: 2901 Chatham Rd, Springfield, IL 62704 Tripadvisor, Official Website
highlights: Unique blend of Texas BBQ and Asian Fusion cuisine Yelp
Complementary appetizer often includes crab rangoon or wings Tripadvisor
Christian owned and operated with a family-oriented environment Official Website
Signature dishes include Honey Spicy Chicken, Beef Bulgogi, and Brisket Official Website
Offers a "video game free zone" to encourage family interaction Official Website
Highly rated for cleanliness and friendly service by the owner-chef Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Jubilee College State Historic Site Illinois Historic Preservation Division
attractions.category: Historic Site, Park Illinois Historic Preservation Division
attractions.description: Jubilee College State Historic Site preserves the remnants of an early 19th-century educational institution founded in 1839 by Philander Chase, the first Episcopal Bishop of Illinois. The site's centerpiece is a restored sandstone Gothic Revival building that once served as a chapel, classroom, and dormitory, illustrating the challenges and triumphs of frontier education Illinois Historic Preservation Division, Wikipedia.
attractions.address: 11817 Jubilee College Road, Brimfield, IL 61517 Illinois Historic Preservation Division
attractions.days_open: Friday and Saturday Illinois Historic Preservation Division
highlights: Restored 1840s sandstone L-shaped building with an Anglican-inspired chapel design Illinois Historic Preservation Division
Guided tours of the historic chapel, schoolrooms, and 19th-century dormitory spaces Illinois Historic Preservation Division
Recreated schoolmaster’s office and library featuring museum exhibits on college history Illinois Historic Preservation Division
Eight-minute orientation video providing historical context for the site Illinois Historic Preservation Division
Beautifully landscaped grounds including an herb garden and a butterfly-attracting flower garden Illinois Historic Preservation Division
Listed on the National Register of Historic Places for its national historical significance Wikipedia

attractions.name: Knight's Action Park and Splash Kingdom (Caribbean Water Adventure)
attractions.description: Knight's Action Park is a 60-acre family entertainment complex in Springfield, Illinois, featuring both a full-scale water park and a variety of dry-land amusement attractions. The park offers a safe and welcoming atmosphere with thrills ranging from high-speed water slides and a wave pool to classic go-karts, mini-golf, and a driving range. Knight's Action Park About
attractions.days_open: Action Park: 7 days a week, year-round (except major holidays). Splash Kingdom: Seasonal (Memorial Day to Labor Day). Knight's Action Park Hours
highlights: Splash Kingdom Waterpark with the 'Big Surf' wave pool and multiple exhilarating slides.
Two scenic 18-hole miniature golf courses featuring winding fairways and obstacles.
Action Park attractions including Go-Karts, The Big Wheel (ferris wheel), and the Paratrooper ride.
A full-scale golf driving range and batting cages for skill-building fun.
The 'Fortress of Fun' outdoor jungle gym and an indoor arcade for younger guests.
The Route 66 Twin Drive-In Theater located on-site for nightly double features.

attractions.name: Lake Springfield
attractions.description: Lake Springfield is a 4,200-acre human-made reservoir that serves as a primary water source and a major recreation hub for central Illinois. Owned and operated by City Water, Light & Power, the lake features 57 miles of shoreline, eight public parks, and numerous facilities for boating, fishing, and water sports. It attracts approximately 600,000 visitors annually and provides a variety of amenities including picnic areas, playgrounds, and specialized recreation centers.
attractions.address: 201 E Lake Shore Dr (CWLP Property Management) / Center Park off Long Bay Drive
highlights: Largest municipally owned lake in Illinois with 4,200 acres of water surface.
Extensive recreation options including boating, fishing, swimming (in designated areas), and water sports.
Home to eight public parks featuring 35 picnic areas, pavilions, and softball diamonds.
Features the 'Bounties of Nature' ADA-compliant playground at Center Park.
Hosts annual events such as the 'Rock the Dock' Independence Day firework celebration.
Proximity to major attractions like the Henson Robinson Zoo and Lincoln Memorial Gardens.

attractions.name: Lanphier Park (Robin Roberts Stadium)
attractions.description: Lanphier Park is a historic municipal park in Springfield, Illinois, primarily known as the home of Robin Roberts Stadium. Established in 1925 on the site of a former city reservoir, it features a 5,200-seat baseball stadium, basketball courts, and picnic areas. The park serves as a community hub for local high school sports and professional collegiate summer league baseball. Springfield Park District, Wikipedia
attractions.days_open: Daily (Park); Monday-Friday (Stadium Box Office) Springfield Park District, Lucky Horseshoes
attractions.phone: 217-544-1751 ext. 1004 (Park District), 217-679-3511 (Lucky Horseshoes) Springfield Park District, Lucky Horseshoes
highlights: Robin Roberts Stadium, a 5,200-seat historic ballpark dating back to 1925.
Home to the Springfield Lucky Horseshoes of the Prospect League.
Selected as the official venue for the inaugural Women's Pro Baseball League season in 2026.
Features one of the few remaining 'turtleback' fields with an elevated infield.
Public amenities including basketball courts, pickleball, and picnic areas north of the stadium.
Named after Hall of Fame pitcher Robin Roberts, a local high school graduate.

attractions.name: Lauterbach Tire & Auto Service (Home of the Lauterbach Giant)
attractions.category: Landmark, Historic Site, Public Art Enjoy Illinois, Roadside America
attractions.description: The Lauterbach Giant is a 25-foot tall fiberglass 'Muffler Man' statue that has stood outside Lauterbach Tire & Auto Service since 1978. Originally designed as a Paul Bunyan figure holding a tire, the statue now patriotically clutches a large American flag and serves as a beloved Route 66 roadside attraction. It is famous for surviving a 2006 tornado that decapitated the figure, which was subsequently repaired and reinstalled by the family-owned business. Enjoy Illinois, Roadside America, ROUTE Magazine
attractions.address: 1569 Wabash Avenue, Springfield, IL 62704 Lauterbach Tire, Enjoy Illinois
highlights: 25-foot tall fiberglass 'Muffler Man' statue Roadside America
Patriotically holds a large American flag Enjoy Illinois
Historic landmark on the original 1926-1930 alignment of Route 66 Tripadvisor
Survived and was repaired after a decapitating tornado in 2006 Enjoy Illinois
One of only three original 'Muffler Men' remaining in Illinois Driving Route 66

attractions.name: Lincoln Home National Historic Site (National Park Service)
attractions.description: Lincoln Home National Historic Site preserves the home where Abraham Lincoln lived with his family from 1844 to 1861, prior to his presidency. The site includes a four-block historic neighborhood with fourteen restored houses from the Lincoln era, offering a glimpse into his life as a husband, father, and neighbor (National Park Service).
attractions.address: 413 South 8th Street, Springfield, IL 62701 (National Park Service)
attractions.days_open: Daily, except for January 1st, Thanksgiving Day, and December 25th (National Park Service).
highlights: Guided tours of the restored 1860s Lincoln Home conducted by Park Rangers (National Park Service)
The four-block historic neighborhood with wooden curbs and period sidewalks (National Park Service)
The Dean House and Arnold House exhibits, focusing on the Lincoln family and their neighbors (National Park Service)
A scaled model of 1860 Springfield located within the Visitor Center (Visit Springfield Illinois)
Living History demonstrations featuring character actors during the summer months (National Park Service)

attractions.name: Lincoln Memorial Garden and Nature Center
attractions.description: Designed by renowned landscape architect Jens Jensen, this 100-acre site is a living memorial to Abraham Lincoln, representing the native landscape he would have known in the Midwest. It features restored prairies, woodlands, and wetlands along the shores of Lake Springfield, offering a serene environment for nature-based education and reflection. Official Website
attractions.address: 2301 East Lake Shore Drive, Springfield, Illinois 62712-8908
attractions.days_open: Garden: Daily; Nature Center: Tuesday–Sunday Official Website
highlights: Six miles of interconnected, scenic hiking trails Official Website
Eight unique stone Council Rings for gathering and reflection Wikipedia
Ostermeier Prairie Center featuring 20 acres of restored tallgrass prairie Wikipedia
Nature Center with educational exhibits and a gift shop Official Website
Seasonal events like the Maple Syrup Festival and Fall Harvest Festival Wikipedia

attractions.name: Lincoln Tomb State Historic Site
attractions.description: The Lincoln Tomb is the final resting place of Abraham Lincoln, the 16th President of the United States, along with his wife Mary Todd Lincoln and three of their four sons. This stunning granite monument, designed by Larkin Mead and completed in 1874, features a 117-foot tall obelisk and various bronze statues honoring Lincoln's legacy. Official Site, Wikipedia
attractions.days_open: Daily (Open year-round except for select holidays) Official Site
highlights: Final resting place of Abraham Lincoln, Mary Todd Lincoln, and three sons (Eddie, Willie, and Tad) Wikipedia
117-foot tall granite obelisk surrounded by bronze statues of Civil War soldiers and sailors Official Site
Bronze bust of Lincoln at the entrance, famous for the tradition of rubbing its nose for good luck Visit Springfield
The public receiving vault where Lincoln's funeral services were held in 1865 National Park Service
Weekly Flag Ceremony held every Tuesday evening from June through August Official Site
Fully accessible interior tours including the burial chamber and several statuettes Official Site

attractions.name: Lincoln's New Salem State Historic Site
attractions.description: Lincoln's New Salem State Historic Site is a faithful reconstruction of the 1830s village where Abraham Lincoln lived for six formative years as a young man. The site features 23 historically furnished log buildings, including homes, stores, and a school, where costumed interpreters demonstrate 19th-century pioneer life. Official Site, Wikipedia
highlights: 23 reconstructed log buildings including the Rutledge Tavern and Lincoln-Berry Store
Costumed historic interpreters demonstrating period activities and trades
Visitor Center with a 17-minute orientation film and museum exhibits
Nearly 700 acres of wooded parkland with 7 miles of hiking trails
The only original structure, the Onstot Cooper Shop, returned to its site in 1922
On-site campground with 200 campsites and outdoor amphitheater for performances

attractions.name: Lost Bridge Trail
attractions.description: The Lost Bridge Trail is a scenic 5-mile multiuse asphalt trail built on the former Baltimore and Ohio Railroad corridor. It connects Springfield's east side to the village of Rochester, passing through dense tree canopies, open farmland, and along a beautifully landscaped lake. The trail is popular for walking, running, bicycling, and rollerblading, offering a well-maintained path for both commuters and recreational users.
attractions.address: 2300 S. Dirksen Parkway (Springfield trailhead) and Walnut St. at SR 29 (Rochester trailhead)
highlights: Connects Springfield to Rochester via a 5-mile paved path
Follows the historic Baltimore and Ohio Railroad corridor
Crosses the South Fork of the Sangamon River and Sugar Creek
Passes through dense tree canopies and scenic open farmland
Wraps around a beautifully landscaped lake near the IDOT complex
Provides access to Rochester Community Park via a paved connector

attractions.name: Mackinaw Valley Vineyard & Winery Mackinaw Valley Vineyard
attractions.category: Vineyard, Winery, and Entertainment Venue Mackinaw Valley Vineyard
attractions.description: Mackinaw Valley Vineyard & Winery is a scenic 86-acre estate situated on a glacial moraine, offering a stunning 30-mile panoramic view of the surrounding countryside. Established in 2003, the vineyard features 15 acres of grapes and a tasting room serving over 24 varieties of award-winning local wines. Visitors can enjoy live music, seasonal festivals, and picturesque walking paths in a peaceful rural setting. Mackinaw Valley Vineyard
attractions.address: 33633 State Route 9, Mackinaw, IL 61755 Mackinaw Valley Vineyard
attractions.days_open: Open year-round except for the month of January and major holidays (Easter, Thanksgiving, Christmas, New Year's Day). Mackinaw Valley Vineyard Hours
highlights: 30-mile panoramic view of the valley from the tasting room and outdoor decks Mackinaw Valley Vineyard
Selection of over 24 award-winning wines, including dry, sweet, and dessert varieties Mackinaw Valley Vineyard
Concerts in the Vineyard series held every Saturday evening from June through September Mackinaw Valley Vineyard Events
Annual signature events like the Grape Stomp & Harvest Festival and Art & Wine Festival Mackinaw Valley Vineyard Festivals
Picturesque 86-acre grounds featuring a lake, gazebo, and miles of vineyard rows Mackinaw Valley Vineyard
Interactive activities including Murder Mystery Dinners and Trivia Game Nights Mackinaw Valley Vineyard Hours

attractions.name: Maldaner's Restaurant & Catering Maldaner's Restaurant
attractions.description: Established in 1884, Maldaner's Restaurant and Catering is one of Springfield's oldest and finest dining establishments. Under the guidance of Chef-Owner Michael Higgins, the restaurant offers contemporary American fine dining in a historical setting, featuring seasonal local and regional ingredients influenced by Mediterranean composition and preparation. Maldaner's Restaurant
highlights: Springfield's oldest restaurant, established in 1884 Maldaner's Restaurant
Green Business Certified, featuring solar panels and rooftop hives for honey Maldaner's Restaurant, Our Changing Life
Focus on 'Slow Food' and farm-to-table local ingredients Maldaner's Restaurant
Located in a historic building in downtown Springfield along the original Route 66 Our Changing Life
First downtown business to install way-finding technology for visually impaired individuals Maldaner's Restaurant

attractions.name: Metamora Courthouse State Historic Site dnrhistoric.illinois.gov
attractions.description: Built in 1845, the Metamora Courthouse served as the Woodford County seat until 1896 and is one of only two surviving courthouses where Abraham Lincoln practiced law on the historic Eighth Judicial Circuit. The two-story brick Greek Revival structure now functions as a museum, with the first floor dedicated to local history and the second floor restored to its 1850s appearance as a county courtroom. dnrhistoric.illinois.gov, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 113 East Partridge Street, Metamora, IL 61548-7021 Enjoy Illinois, Tripadvisor
attractions.days_open: Saturdays (Official); Tuesday – Saturday (Seasonal/Traditional) dnrhistoric.illinois.gov, Enjoy Illinois
highlights: One of only two surviving courthouses on the historic Eighth Judicial Circuit traveled by Abraham Lincoln. dnrhistoric.illinois.gov
First-floor museum featuring artifacts of early local history and the 1850s court system. dnrhistoric.illinois.gov
Second-floor courtroom restored to its original 1850s appearance where Lincoln handled over 70 cases. Wikipedia
Annual 'Christmas in the Courthouse' celebration held the first Friday in December. dnrhistoric.illinois.gov
Listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 1978. Wikipedia

attractions.name: Monty's Submarines (Home of the Beefeater)
attractions.description: Monty's Submarines is a popular local sandwich institution in Springfield, Illinois, known for its hearty oven-toasted subs and fresh ingredients. Its signature "Beefeater" sub is a fan-favorite, featuring heaps of delicately seasoned roast beef topped with tangy cheddar cheese on a toasted roll. Monty's Submarines, Yelp
highlights: Signature "Beefeater" roast beef and cheddar sub
Oven-toasted sandwiches made with fresh ingredients
Multiple locations across Springfield serving the community for years
Local favorite for quick and hearty lunches near the State Fairgrounds
Online ordering available for convenient pickup

attractions.name: Norb Andy's Tabarin
attractions.description: Norb Andy's Tabarin is a legendary basement tavern located in the historic Virgil Hickox House, Springfield's oldest residential building (built in 1839). Established in 1937, it is famous for its "horseshoe" sandwiches, traditional nautical-themed decor, and its legacy as a favorite gathering spot for Illinois state legislators and journalists. SangamonLink, Illinois Times
highlights: Housed in the historic Virgil Hickox House, built in 1839 and listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
Famous for serving the 'horseshoe' sandwich, utilizing a recipe linked to the original Leland Hotel.
A storied past as a Prohibition-era speakeasy and the original site of the Sangamo Club.
Long-standing tradition as a popular 'hangout' for Illinois politicians and professionals since 1937.
Unique basement atmosphere featuring dark wood, nautical decor, and cozy seating.
Hosts popular community events such as trivia nights and live local music performances.

attractions.name: Norb Andy's Tabarin Wikipedia, CMH Gourmand
attractions.category: Restaurant / Historic Site Wikipedia, Illinois Times
attractions.description: Norb Andy’s Tabarin is a historic tavern and restaurant located in the basement of the Virgil Hickox House, an 1839 Italianate mansion in downtown Springfield. Known as a long-standing gathering place for legislators and journalists, it features a cozy, dim atmosphere with nautical-themed decor. The establishment is famous for its variations of the "horseshoe" sandwich and has a rich history that includes serving as a speakeasy during Prohibition. Wikipedia, Illinois Times, CMH Gourmand
attractions.address: 518 E Capitol Ave, Springfield, IL 62701, USA Wanderlog, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Obed & Isaac's Microbrewery and Eatery
attractions.description: Obed & Isaac's Microbrewery and Eatery is a local favorite housed in a beautifully preserved 1850s Italianate mansion in downtown Springfield. The establishment features a microbrewery located in a historic carriage house, an award-winning bocce beer garden, and an adjacent cidery. It is renowned for its selection of craft beers, family recipes, and the locally famous Horseshoe sandwich. (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Every day except Christmas and Thanksgiving (Official Website)
highlights: Housed in the historic Booth-Grundendike mansion, originally built over 150 years ago.
Award-winning Bocce Beer Garden featuring outdoor games and a pet-friendly atmosphere.
On-site microbrewery producing a variety of craft beers in the original Obed Lewis carriage house.
Features the 7th Street Cidery next door, specializing in ciders, meads, and cocktails.
Famous for serving the 'Horseshoe' sandwich, a quintessential Springfield regional dish.
Host of various community events including the Moonlight Market and 'Yappy Days' for dogs.

attractions.name: Old State Capitol State Historic Site
attractions.description: The Old State Capitol is a meticulous reconstruction of Illinois' fifth statehouse, serving as the seat of government from 1839 to 1876. It is famously associated with Abraham Lincoln, who practiced law, served as a legislator, and delivered his iconic 'House Divided' speech within its walls.
attractions.address: 1 Old State Capitol Plaza, Springfield, IL 62701
highlights: Site of Abraham Lincoln's 'House Divided' speech in 1858
Reconstruction of Illinois' fifth statehouse (1839–1876)
Location where Barack Obama announced his 2007 presidential candidacy
Greek Revival style limestone architecture designed by John Rague
Served as the final laying-in-state location for President Lincoln in 1865
Underground parking garage available beneath the plaza

attractions.name: Old State Capitol State Historic Site (formerly Sangamon County Courthouse)
attractions.description: The Old State Capitol is a meticulous reconstruction of Illinois' fifth statehouse, serving as the seat of government from 1840 to 1876 and later as the Sangamon County Courthouse until 1966. It is famously the site where Abraham Lincoln delivered his 'House Divided' speech and practiced law before the Illinois Supreme Court. Today, it stands as a National Historic Landmark, offering visitors a glimpse into the mid-19th century through restored legislative chambers and judicial offices.
attractions.address: 1 Old State Capitol Plaza, Springfield, IL 62701
highlights: The Representatives Hall, where Abraham Lincoln delivered his 1858 'House Divided' speech Wikipedia
The Illinois Supreme Courtroom where Lincoln argued hundreds of cases Visit Springfield
Governor's Office and Secretary of State's Office restored to their 1860s appearance Illinois DNR
The site of Barack Obama's 2007 presidential candidacy announcement Wikipedia
Stunning Greek Revival architecture including a central dome and porticoes National Park Service

attractions.name: Oliver P. Parks Telephone Museum
attractions.description: The Oliver P. Parks Telephone Museum is a specialized institution that showcases a vast collection of antique telephones and related communication technology. It illustrates the evolution of the telephone from early wood wall models and magneto phones to touch-tone technology and 1980s acrylic versions (Texas Eagle, YouTube).
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Note: Reported as permanently closed by some sources) (Texas Eagle, WhichMuseum)
highlights: Extensive collection of antique wood wall-mounted telephones (YouTube)
Early hand-crank magneto phones used in rural areas (YouTube)
Iconic rotary dial and "candlestick" style desktop phones (YouTube)
Historical telephone switchboards used by manual operators (YouTube)
Specialty 1980s clear acrylic light-up telephones (YouTube)
Displays detailing the technological transition from analog to digital communication (YouTube)

attractions.name: Otis B. Duncan Park (formerly Douglas Park)
attractions.description: Otis B. Duncan Park, formerly Douglas Park, is a 26-acre community space established in 1920 and renamed in 2020 to honor the highest-ranking Black officer in the American Expeditionary Forces during WWI. The park features a wide array of recreational facilities including Springfield's first disc golf course, a modern adventure playground with a zip line, and several pickleball courts. It serves as a central hub for community gatherings, offering scenic green spaces and a historic bandshell for performances and events.
highlights: 18-Hole Disc Golf Course (Springfield's first, built in 2000) Springfield Park District
Adventure Playground featuring a zip line and outdoor musical instruments Springfield Park District
Eight Pickleball Courts available for public use Springfield Park District
Scenic Bandshell for outdoor concerts and performances Springfield Park District
Outdoor Fitness Court for body-weight routines Springfield Park District
Rentable Picnic Shelters for community events and celebrations Springfield Park District

attractions.name: President Abraham Lincoln Springfield - a DoubleTree by Hilton Hotel
attractions.description: The President Abraham Lincoln Springfield - a DoubleTree by Hilton Hotel is a premier upscale hotel located in the heart of historic downtown Springfield. It features 310 guest rooms, extensive meeting space, and is the only hotel connected to the Bank of Springfield (BOS) Center via an underground concourse. Hilton, Cvent
attractions.days_open: Open daily (7 days a week) Yelp
highlights: Connected to the Bank of Springfield (BOS) Center via an underground concourse. Wikipedia
Walking distance to the Abraham Lincoln Presidential Library and Museum and Lincoln Home National Historic Site. Expedia
Features over 15,000 square feet of flexible meeting and event space. Cvent
On-site dining options include Lindsay’s Restaurant and The Globe Tavern. Expedia
Amenities include a heated indoor pool and a 24-hour fitness center. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: President Abraham Lincoln Springfield - a DoubleTree by Hilton Hotel (formerly Renaissance Springfield Hotel)
attractions.description: This 12-story, full-service hotel is located in the heart of historic downtown Springfield and is the only hotel connected to the Bank of Springfield Center via an underground concourse. It features over 13,000 sq. ft. of flexible meeting space, a fitness center, and an indoor pool, while offering easy access to major Lincoln-related historic sites. Hilton, Wikipedia
highlights: Connected via tunnel to the Bank of Springfield (BOS) Convention Center Wikipedia
Signature warm DoubleTree chocolate chip cookie upon arrival Hilton
Heated indoor pool and 24-hour fitness center Tripadvisor
Walking distance to the Abraham Lincoln Presidential Library and Museum Hilton
On-site dining at Lindsay's Restaurant and The Globe Tavern Hilton
Complimentary shuttle to AMTRAK and Abraham Lincoln Capital Airport Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Riverside Park
attractions.description: Established in 1965, Riverside Park is a sprawling 470-acre recreational area situated on the far north side of Springfield, Illinois. The park is bordered by the Sangamon River and Carpenter Park, featuring a diverse landscape of upland and lowland forests. It offers a wide range of amenities including a public campground, hiking trails, a BMX track, and several baseball fields. Visit Springfield
attractions.days_open: Daily (Park); May 1 - November 1 (Campground) Springfield Park District
highlights: 470 acres of scenic upland and lowland forest along the Sangamon River.
Public campground with over 60 sites offering various utility hookups.
Dedicated BMX track for riders of all skill levels, managed with the Springfield BMX Club.
Extensive trail system for both hiking and horseback riding.
Eight baseball fields located in the river's flood plain.
Designated off-leash dog run area.

attractions.name: Robbie's Restaurant
attractions.description: Robbie's Restaurant was a long-standing downtown staple located in the historic C.M. Smith Building, where Abraham Lincoln once worked. Known for its lunchtime classics and casual atmosphere, it famously hosted live jazz sessions and served a variety of unique sandwiches and homemade pies. Although the original restaurant has closed, the site remains a significant part of the Old State Capitol Plaza's culinary and historical heritage.
attractions.address: 4 S. Old State Capitol Plaza, Springfield, IL 62701
highlights: Housed in the historic C.M. Smith Building, where Abraham Lincoln drafted his first inaugural address.
Regularly hosted 'Uptown Friday Night' jazz events sponsored by the Springfield Area Arts Council.
Famous for its signature 'Weird Sarah' open-faced roast beef 'salwich.'
Renowned for its thick and creamy broccoli cheese soup, often cited as a local favorite.
Featured a daily Coney Special including two hot dogs, hand-cut fries, and a drink.
Offered outdoor patio seating with a direct view of the Old State Capitol.

attractions.name: Route 66 MotorHeads Bar & Grill, Museum & Entertainment Complex
attractions.description: Route 66 MotorHeads Bar & Grill, Museum & Entertainment Complex is a vibrant destination in Springfield that pays homage to the iconic "Mother Road." It features a themed restaurant and bar adorned with vintage memorabilia, an extensive motor museum, and a large entertainment space hosting car shows and live music.
highlights: Largest Route 66 sign in the parking lot
"Big Ron" giant fiberglass statue
Extensive collection of petroliana and vintage cars, including a 1969 Z/28 Camaro
Weekly food specials like Prime Rib and Fried Chicken nights
MotorDome event center and gaming lounge
Gift shop featuring Route 66 and MotorHeads merchandise

attractions.name: Route 66 Twin Drive-In Theater Official Website
attractions.category: Entertainment / Cinema / Historic Site Enjoy Illinois
attractions.description: The Route 66 Twin Drive-In is a nostalgic twin-screen movie theater located along the historic Mother Road in Springfield. It offers a family-friendly environment where visitors can enjoy double-feature films under the stars with modern FM radio sound and a comprehensive snack bar featuring kosher options. Official Website, Visit Springfield
attractions.address: 1700 Knights Recreation Drive, Springfield, IL 62711 Official Website, Tripadvisor
attractions.days_open: Seasonal: Weekends only (April-May & Aug-Sept); Wednesday-Sunday (Memorial Day-Aug 9) Official Website

Open caption nights on Fridays for enhanced accessibility Ticket Site
Located adjacent to Knight's Action Park for a full day of family entertainment Illinois Route 66 Scenic Byway

attractions.name: Saint Louis Zoological Park (Saint Louis Zoo)
attractions.description: The Saint Louis Zoo is a world-class destination located in Forest Park, dedicated to animal management, research, and conservation. It is home to over 14,000 animals across 500 species and is one of the few free zoos in the United States.
attractions.address: One Government Drive, St. Louis, MO 63110
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed Thanksgiving Day [9-2], Christmas Day, and New Year's Day)
