Penguin & Puffin Coast (open-air habitat)
McDonnell Polar Bear Point
Zooline Railroad (narrated tour)
Michael and Quirsis Riney Primate Canopy Trails
Sea Lion Show and Stingrays at Caribbean Cove

attractions.name: Saputo's Italian Restaurant
attractions.description: Saputo's is a family-owned and operated landmark in downtown Springfield, serving authentic Southern Italian cuisine since 1948. For four generations, the restaurant has been a local tradition known for its handcrafted family recipes, including homemade pastas, veal, and chicken specialties.
highlights: Traditional Southern Italian family recipes passed down through four generations.
Signature dishes like Baked Lasagna with Mafaldine noodles and Italian Breaded Steak.
Established in 1948, making it one of the oldest family-owned restaurants in the city.
Hand-breaded Shrimp Scampi and homemade Veal and Chicken Parmigiana.
A classic "supper club" atmosphere popular with locals and traveling dignitaries.
Famous house-made Italian salad dressing and toasted ravioli.

attractions.name: Shea's Gas Station Museum
attractions.description: Shea’s Gas Station Museum is a legendary Route 66 landmark that served as a Texaco and later Marathon station from 1946 to 1982. Transformed into a museum by Hall of Fame inductee Bill Shea, it was restored and reopened by the City of Springfield in May 2024 to preserve its rich automotive history and iconic roadside Americana (Visit Springfield, Pride Journeys).
highlights: Restored 1946 Texaco and Marathon gas station building (Visit Springfield)
Reproductions of original vintage signage on the exterior and interior (TheRoute-66.com)
Extensive collection of gas station and oil company memorabilia (Tripadvisor)
Outdoor display of colorful old-fashioned gas pumps and period vehicles (Easy Access Springfield)
Historical marker honoring Route 66 Hall of Fame inductee Bill Shea (YouTube)

attractions.name: Springfield Union Station (Illinois)
attractions.description: Union Station is a historic former train station in Springfield, Illinois, originally opened in 1898 by the Illinois Central Railroad. Now part of the Abraham Lincoln Presidential Library and Museum complex, the Richardson Romanesque-style building features a rebuilt 110-foot clock tower and serves as a significant architectural landmark within the central historic district. Wikipedia), ALPLM
attractions.days_open: Daily (Museum campus) / Union Station building is currently closed to the public ALPLM
highlights: Richardson Romanesque architecture designed by Francis T. Bacon Wikipedia)
Rebuilt 110-foot clock tower with Arabic numerals and 33 programmed chimes ALPLM
Original quarter-sawn oak perimeter benches and paneling ALPLM
Adjacent Union Square Park featuring 'A Greater Task' Lincoln statue ALPLM
Mary Lincoln's flower garden located in the adjacent park ALPLM
Listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 1978 Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Springfield Visitors Center (Official)
attractions.description: The Springfield Visitors Center serves as the official gateway for travelers exploring the capital of Illinois, particularly those trekking the historic Route 66. It provides comprehensive travel resources, including specialized maps, brochures, and the popular 'Living Legends and Landmarks of Route 66' Explorer Passport. Visitors can receive expert advice from staff and explore on-site historical exhibits like the replication of the Lincoln-Herndon Law Office. Visit Springfield, Illinois Route 66 Scenic Byway
highlights: Living Legends and Landmarks of Route 66 Explorer Passport Visit Springfield
Comprehensive Historic Route 66 and Springfield city maps Visit Springfield
Replication of the Lincoln-Herndon Law Office and historical Post Office Tripadvisor
Gift shop featuring unique Springfield and Route 66 souvenirs Wheree
Expert travel assistance and personalized itinerary planning Visit Springfield

attractions.name: Steak 'n Shake - S Dirksen Pkwy
attractions.description: Steak 'n Shake is a classic American restaurant brand founded in Illinois in 1934, famous for its hand-crafted Steakburgers and hand-dipped milkshakes. This location serves as a modern link to the chain's historic Route 66 roots, offering a nostalgic diner experience for travelers along the Mother Road. Guests can enjoy premium 100% beef burgers and a variety of specialty shakes in a traditional drive-in and dine-in setting.
highlights: Signature 100% beef Steakburgers made without preservatives or artificial ingredients.
Famous hand-dipped milkshakes available in various classic and specialty flavors.
Historic connection to the original Route 66 brand heritage founded by Gus Belt.
Drive-thru and dine-in service options for travelers and locals.
Classic 'TakHomaSak' curbside and carryout service tradition.
Iconic thin and crispy fries served as a signature side.

attractions.name: The Ariston Cafe
attractions.description: The Ariston Cafe is the oldest continuously operated restaurant along the entire stretch of Route 66, originally founded in 1924 by Greek immigrant Pete Adam. Known for its traditional American service and diverse menu, the historic establishment has been a staple for travelers and locals alike for over a century. The building was added to the U.S. National Register of Historic Places in 2006 and remains a celebrated landmark of the "Mother Road."
attractions.address: 413 Old Rte 66 N, Litchfield, IL 62056, United States
highlights: Recognized as the oldest continuously operated restaurant on Route 66 Official Website
Listed on the U.S. National Register of Historic Places since 2006 Wikipedia
Famous for its massive dessert tray featuring a wide variety of homemade cakes and pies Visit Litchfield
Offers a diverse menu including hand-cut steaks, fresh seafood, and Italian specialties Official Website
Most entrees include access to a comprehensive soup and salad bar TripAdvisor
Preserves historic 1930s architecture with Art Deco interior elements Wikipedia

attractions.name: The Curve Inn
attractions.category: Bar & Grill / Entertainment Venue Enjoy Illinois
attractions.description: The Curve Inn is an iconic Route 66 bar and grill in Springfield, Illinois, originally built in 1932 and renamed in 1945. It is celebrated as a premier live music venue featuring a state-of-the-art beer garden stage and a rich history that includes a "scandalous" past of gambling and entertainment. The establishment remains a community staple, frequently voted as one of the best bars in Central Illinois and recognized as a Living Legend on the Route 66 Scenic Byway Illinois Times, Curve Inn.
attractions.address: 3219 South 6th Street Road, Springfield, IL 62703 Visit Springfield
highlights: Designated as a 'Living Legend & Landmark of Route 66' on the historic scenic byway Visit Springfield
Features a large, state-of-the-art beer garden stage hosting local, regional, and national acts Curve Inn
Member of the official Springfield Horseshoe Trail, serving the city's signature open-faced sandwich Visit Springfield
Frequently voted as the '#1 Bar' and best live music venue in Central Illinois Curve Inn
Rich historic building dating back to 1932 with a colorful local history Illinois Times
Strong community presence, raising thousands of dollars annually for local charities Curve Inn

attractions.name: The Feed Store
attractions.description: The Feed Store is a charming, vintage-style eatery located in a historic Victorian building in downtown Springfield. Known for its Victorian decor and warm ambiance, it has long been a favorite destination for tourists and state workers seeking hearty homemade soups and sandwiches. The restaurant gained national attention as a frequent lunch spot for former President Barack Obama during his time as an Illinois state senator (Ordinary Time, Wanderlog).
highlights: Famous for its diverse selection of homemade soups, including local favorites like Wisconsin Cheese and Mushroom Bisque (Tripadvisor).
Known for classic American sandwiches such as the "Unholy Cow" and corned beef on rye (Wanderlog).
Housed in a meticulously rehabbed Lincoln-era building with high ceilings and Victorian charm (Easy Access Springfield).
Historic significance as a regular dining spot for political figures, including Barack Obama (Illinois Times).
Traditional accompaniment of signature pickle spears and orange slices with every meal (Springfield Business Journal).

attractions.name: The Lincoln Depot (Great Western Railroad Depot)
attractions.description: The Lincoln Depot, also known as the Great Western Railroad Depot, is the historic site where President-elect Abraham Lincoln delivered his emotional Farewell Address to the citizens of Springfield on February 11, 1861, before departing for his inauguration in Washington, D.C. Today, the building serves as the home of the Noll Law Office on the upper floors, while the ground floor features a self-guided museum dedicated to the events of that day and Lincoln's connection to the site. The Lincoln Depot, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 930 East Monroe Street, Springfield, IL 62701, USA
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Weekends and Holidays by appointment/contact for availability). The Lincoln Depot
highlights: Site of Abraham Lincoln's 1861 Farewell Address to Springfield. Wikipedia
Ground floor self-guided museum featuring historical exhibits. The Lincoln Depot
Restored 1852 brick structure, originally built by the Great Western Railroad. Enjoy Illinois
Listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 2014. The Lincoln Depot
Historical marker erected in 1966 commemorating Lincoln's departure. Wikipedia
Located just two blocks from the Lincoln Home National Historic Site. NPS

attractions.name: The Oscar Tortola Group Diamond Taproom
attractions.description: The Oscar Tortola Group Diamond Taproom is a full-service restaurant and bar directly connected to Lake Elsinore Diamond Stadium, offering a cozy indoor space and a giant outdoor party deck. It features a brand-new kitchen and bar area overlooking the baseball field, serving refreshingly unique drinks and upscale ballpark cuisine since 1994.
attractions.address: 500 Diamond Dr, Lake Elsinore, CA 92530, USA
attractions.days_open: Thursday - Sunday (during homestand weekends and seasonally in the offseason) (MiLB.com)
highlights: Newly renovated bar and kitchen area with field-side views (Diamond Taproom)
Giant party deck for outdoor dining and socializing (Chamber of Commerce)
In-house distillery and curated craft beer selection (MLB.com)
Menu featuring creative items like elote fries, mac n cheese burgers, and decadent desserts (Postmates)
Brand-new luxury Speakeasy suite for private parties up to 30 people (MiLB.com)

attractions.name: The Pharmacy Gallery and Art Space
attractions.description: The Pharmacy Gallery and Art Space is a non-profit, artist-run organization that cultivates and celebrates the expressions of multidimensional artists. Established in 2011, it serves as a catalyst for advancing contemporary art through exhibitions, music events, and community workshops. The Pharmacy Gallery & Art Space, Visit Springfield Illinois
attractions.days_open: Friday, Saturday (Regular hours); Tuesday (Open Studio) The Pharmacy Gallery & Art Space, Green Dodge
attractions.phone: 217-381-8910 (Text or leave message) NPR Illinois, The Pharmacy Gallery & Art Space
highlights: Rotating contemporary art exhibitions featuring juried member artists The Pharmacy Gallery & Art Space
Monthly Jazz performances and diverse musical events Wanderboat AI
Weekly Open Studio sessions for community drawing and painting Green Dodge
Special Route 66-themed exhibits, including Bob Waldmire's work NPR Illinois
Spoken word and poetry open mic nights The Pharmacy Gallery & Art Space
Interactive art installations and community-driven cultural events Enjoy Illinois

attractions.name: Thomas Rees Memorial Carillon
attractions.category: Historic Site, Landmark, Musical Instrument, Park Attraction
attractions.description: The Thomas Rees Memorial Carillon is one of the world's largest and finest carillons, featuring 67 cast bronze bells housed in a 132-foot Brutalist-style tower within Washington Park. Dedicated in 1962, it serves as a premier musical instrument where carillonneurs perform a diverse repertoire ranging from classical to popular music. Visitors can enjoy free public concerts on the park grounds or take guided tours that provide scenic views of the city from the tower's observation decks. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.days_open: Wednesday - Sunday (May - September); Saturdays & Sundays (April, October - December); Winter (January - March) by appointment for tours (Official Website, Springfield Park District)
highlights: One of the largest carillons in the world with 67 bronze bells (Springfield Park District).
Host of the annual International Carillon Festival featuring world-renowned carillonneurs (Visit Springfield Illinois).
132-foot Brutalist-style tower with three observation decks (Wikipedia).
Regular free public concerts scheduled throughout the year (Official Website).
Seasonal community events like the Jack-O-Lantern Spectacular and Art Spectacular (Official Website).

attractions.name: Vinegar Hill Mall
attractions.category: Historic Site, Shopping, and Dining Mall
attractions.description: Vinegar Hill Mall is a historic property in Springfield, Illinois, originally built in 1908 as a commercial laundry facility and later repurposed into a retail and dining space in the 1980s. Currently owned by Conn's Hospitality Group, the mall houses Trish & Mary's Public House, an English-style pub, and serves as a venue for community events and markets.
highlights: Home to Trish & Mary's Public House, an English-style pub specializing in imported beers and soccer matches.
Historic 1908 building originally used as a commercial laundry facility.
Host of community events like the Kringle Christmas Market and adult book fairs.
Features a unique, relaxed atmosphere with brick-paved courtyard and patio seating.
Located just two blocks south of the Illinois State Capitol in the historic Vinegar Hill neighborhood.
Underwent a major two-year renovation project starting in 2021 to revitalize the space.

attractions.name: Washington Park Botanical Garden
attractions.description: The Washington Park Botanical Garden is a 20-acre tranquil oasis in Central Illinois featuring more than 1,200 species of plants across ten distinct garden areas and a newly renovated 50-foot glass conservatory dome. Visitors can explore tropical flora from Africa, Asia, and South America indoors, or enjoy outdoor highlights like a 5,000-plant rose garden and seasonal floral displays. (Springfield Park District, Woodward Community Media)
highlights: Newly renovated 50-foot diameter glass Conservatory Dome with tropical and exotic plants (Woodward Community Media)
Rose Garden featuring 5,000 plants, the largest of its kind in central Illinois (Springfield Park District)
Themed outdoor areas including a cactus garden, iris garden, and Betty Mood Smith Rockery (Springfield Park District)
Annual Spring Floral Display and seasonal exhibits like Easter lilies and poinsettias (Springfield Park District)
Educational workshops and free school tours for all grade levels (Springfield Park District)
Extensive collection of over 1,200 plant species (Springfield Park District)

attractions.name: Washington Park Botanical Garden (Official name in the Springfield, IL area)
attractions.description: The Washington Park Botanical Garden is a 20-acre horticultural attraction featuring more than 1,200 species of plants. It serves as a tranquil urban oasis with themed gardens, a 50-foot conservatory dome, and seasonal floral displays.
highlights: 50-foot diameter glass conservatory dome with tropical and economically important plants
5,000-plant Rose Garden, the largest of its kind in central Illinois
Themed gardens including Cactus, Iris, Rock, and Roman Cultural gardens
9,000 square feet of indoor greenhouse and exhibit hall space
Seasonal floral displays including the Spring Floral Show and Orchid exhibits
Adjacent to the historic Thomas Rees Memorial Carillon

attractions.name: Wm. Van's Café
attractions.description: Wm. Van's Café is a local, family-owned coffee house located in the historic Vinegar Hill Mall in Springfield, Illinois. It offers a unique mix of old and new, creativity and craft, focusing on quality drip coffee and an espresso bar experience. The cafe is housed in a historic mid-19th-century home that was saved and relocated to its current site. Conn's Hospitality Group
highlights: Located in the historic Vinegar Hill Mall
Family-owned and operated by Conn's Hospitality Group
Expertly crafted espresso and drip coffee drinks
Housed in a relocated historic 1850s home
Warm, inviting atmosphere with a mix of vintage and modern decor
Offers a selection of pastries and light cafe fare

attractions.name: World War II Illinois Veterans Memorial (Official Website)
attractions.description: The World War II Illinois Veterans Memorial honors the 987,000 men and women from Illinois who served during the war and the 22,000 who gave their lives (Official Website). Its focal point is a 22-ton white concrete world globe flanked by black granite walls engraved with major battles and historical dates (Illinois DNR). The site serves as a place of reflection and education, featuring poignant quotations from military and national leaders (Visit Springfield).
highlights: A 12-foot diameter white concrete globe representing the global scale of the conflict (Illinois DNR)
Over 70 stainless steel buttons marking significant battles and events across the world (Visit Springfield)
Two 100-foot-long black granite walls flanking the globe with engraved battle details (Illinois DNR)
Engraved quotations from military leaders and Presidents Franklin D. Roosevelt and Harry S. Truman (Illinois DNR)
A central plaza featuring thousands of personalized bricks honoring individual Illinois veterans (Illinois Times)
Located within the historic Oak Ridge Cemetery near other major state military memorials (Veterans Illinois)
